target_criticism: The central benchmark evidence is thin and self-referential: determinism results come from a single GPU class, a single model, and small cohorts of 29 and 42 validators, while supporting references appear to point to internal project files rather than independently retrievable artifacts.
what_this_changes: Public URLs address the claim that the static benchmark artifacts are only internal project files.
what_this_does_not_change: The benchmark evidence remains narrow: one active model family, one pinned SGLang/Modal profile, and small saved validator cohorts.
materially_different_measurement_needed: Second public model profile rerun on the same frozen inputs.
Cross-hardware replay beyond the current pinned H100/H200-style profile.
Larger or newer live validator cohorts.
Human or deterministic baseline labels sufficient to evaluate usefulness rather than repeatability.
safe_replacement_sentence: The benchmark artifacts cited here are publicly retrievable and hash-checkable, but the current empirical claim remains deliberately narrow: same-stack replayability under one pinned Qwen/SGLang profile on the saved XRPL and PFT validator cohorts.
do_not_claim: The published artifacts establish cross-model reproducibility, heterogeneous hardware replay, model superiority over deterministic or human baselines, or production-sufficient AI governance.
integration_target: One compact public-artifact sentence or appendix line; no new breadth claim.
